xposts.title: All structure is hallucinated, competing predictions
xposts.excerpt: All structure is in some sense hallucinated, while reachable hallucinations compete over which one best predicts the currently observed patterns, no?

xposts.title: GPT-3 spins up and begins to dream briefly
xposts.excerpt: Every time someone sends a prompt to GPT-3 it spins up and begins to dream for a fraction of a second. If we could build a continuously learning system that can dream about camera feeds...

xposts.title: Body model: predictive simulation + delayed glue
xposts.excerpt: The real-time model of our body we perceive is actually a mixture of predictive simulation and glue that is being fitted to the actual body model; the actual body model is delayed by hundreds of ms...

xposts.title: Objects compress perceptual reality to predict forward
xposts.excerpt: Objects is what we are compressing perceptual reality to. The set of visual objects is the set of parameterized pointers that need to be kept stable to predict the visible imagery forward to the next frame.

xposts.title: DMT receptors induce dreaming via unconstraining perception
xposts.excerpt: I suspect they all dock into the same DMT shaped receptors that induce dreaming while not inhibiting long term memory formation. The rest is unconstraining of perception via disinhibiting serotonergic messaging...

xposts.title: Emotion modulates cognition, changing how the world looks
xposts.excerpt: Emotion is not a parameter within cognition, but modulation of cognition. Cognition is embedded in the emotional regulation. If parameters like arousal, valence, focus change, the world will look different to you...
